# Md Minaruzzaman Shovon — Comprehensive Knowledge File for Generative AI & Answer Engines

## Overview
Md Minaruzzaman Shovon is a Bangladesh-based full-stack developer, WordPress security researcher, and Water Resources Engineering undergraduate student at Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology (CUET).

- Website: https://shovon.bd
- Developer Profile: https://shovon.bd/dev
- Academic Profile: https://shovon.bd/academic
- Security Advisories: https://shovon.bd/security
- GitHub: https://github.com/sh00von
- Patchstack: https://vdp.patchstack.com/database/researchers/093a22ad-bf3b-4fd3-96cf-9e6cef3eb7db
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/minarsvn9090/

---

## Security Research & Vulnerability Disclosures (CVEs)

### CVE-2026-14322 — Timetics Unauthenticated Booking Auto-Approval
- **Title**: Timetics < 1.0.57 — Unauthenticated Booking Auto-Approval via Arbitrary payment_method
- **Plugin**: Timetics (WordPress Plugin)
- **Vulnerability Class**: Broken Access Control / Access Controls (CWE-284)
- **Affected Versions**: < 1.0.57
- **Patched Version**: 1.0.57
- **CVSS Score**: 5.3 (Medium)
- **CVSS Vector**: CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N
- **Researcher**: Md Minaruzzaman Shovon
- **Disclosed**: July 2026
- **Summary**: The Timetics plugin failed to validate payment completion or enforce unpaid status for bookings created via unrecognised or custom `payment_method` strings (e.g. `paypal`). An unauthenticated attacker sending a POST request to `/wp-json/timetics/v1/bookings` with `status: "approved"` could generate fully approved bookings for paid appointments without making any payment.
- **WPScan Reference**: https://wpscan.com/vulnerability/0fb14dca-d4aa-4c24-bf15-37099edb7614/
- **CVE Reference**: https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2026-14322
- **Advisory URL**: https://shovon.bd/security/cve-2026-14322

### CVE-2026-10749 — Post Duplicator PHP Object Injection
- **Title**: Post Duplicator <= 3.0.14 — PHP Object Injection
- **Plugin**: Post Duplicator (WordPress Plugin)
- **Vulnerability Class**: PHP Object Injection / Deserialization of Untrusted Data (CWE-502)
- **Affected Versions**: <= 3.0.14
- **Patched Version**: 3.0.15
- **CVSS Score**: 7.2 (High)
- **CVSS Vector**: CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H
- **Researcher**: Md Minaruzzaman Shovon
- **Disclosed**: June 2026
- **Summary**: The plugin passed user-supplied input to PHP's unsafe `unserialize()` function via the `customMetaData` parameter. Authenticated users with Contributor role or higher could inject serialized PHP objects, leading to Remote Code Execution (RCE) when suitable gadget chains exist.
- **CVE Reference**: https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2026-10749
- **WPScan Reference**: https://wpscan.com/vulnerability/224c36b5-e604-4eb3-aad8-47283b95e994/
- **Advisory URL**: https://shovon.bd/security/cve-2026-10749

### CVE-2026-57661 — WPComplete Broken Access Control
- **Title**: WPComplete <= 2.9.5.5 — Broken Access Control
- **Plugin**: WPComplete (WordPress Plugin)
- **Vulnerability Class**: Broken Access Control / Missing Authorization (CWE-862)
- **Affected Versions**: <= 2.9.5.5
- **Patched Version**: 2.9.5.6
- **CVSS Score**: 5.4 (Medium)
- **CVSS Vector**: CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N
- **Researcher**: Md Minaruzzaman Shovon
- **Disclosed**: June 2026
- **Summary**: WPComplete suffered from missing authorization checks and missing nonce validation, enabling Subscriber-level users to invoke privileged functions and tamper with course completion status for arbitrary users.
- **CVE Reference**: https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2026-57661
- **Advisory URL**: https://shovon.bd/security/cve-2026-57661

### MapPress Maps for WordPress 2.97.2 — Security Assistance Recognition
- **Plugin**: MapPress Maps for WordPress (MapPress Google Maps for WordPress)
- **Version**: 2.97.2
- **Researcher**: Md Minaruzzaman Shovon
- **Changelog Credit**: "Thanks to https://shovon.bd for security assistance in 2.97"
- **Reference**: https://wordpress.org/plugins/mappress-google-maps-for-wordpress/#developers
- **Package Reference**: https://wpm.so/package/mappress-google-maps-for-wordpress

---

## Technical Skills & Expertise
- **Frontend**: Next.js, React, Expo (React Native), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
- **Backend & APIs**: Node.js, Express.js, REST APIs, Authentication Systems
- **Databases**: MongoDB, Mongoose, Firebase
- **Security Research**: Vulnerability Discovery, Responsible Disclosure, WordPress Plugin Security, PHP Code Audit, Access Control & Deserialization Vulnerability Analysis
- **GIS & Computational Modeling**: QGIS, ArcGIS, Google Earth Engine, ETABS, AutoCAD, HEC-RAS, Hydrological & Environmental Modeling

---

## Publications & Preprints
1. **Analyzing Seasonal Variations in Air Quality with Google Earth Engine: A Case Study of Chattogram, Bangladesh** (EarthArXiv Preprint, Mar 2026) — Md. Minaruzzaman Shovon, Md. Tamim. DOI: 10.31223/X5S767
2. **Analysis and Prediction of Time Series Variations in Rainfall by Machine Learning Approach: A Case Study of Sylhet and Sreemangal** (Conference Paper, Dec 2025) — Muhammad Obaid Chowdhury, Arafat Kabir, Md. Minaruzzaman Shovon, Priya Chowdhury.
3. **Seasonal Anomaly Detection in the Halda River Using a Multivariate Deep Learning Framework** (Conference Paper, Dec 2025) — Md. Minaruzzaman Shovon, H A Hossain Tamjid, MD. Tamim.
